title: September 1922
content:
We�ll to the weeds no more,
    The laurels are all cut,
    The bowers are bare of bay
    That once the Muses wore;
    The year draws in the day
    And soon will evening shut:
    The laurels all are cut,
    We�ll to the woods no more.
    Oh we�ll no more, no more
    To the leafy woods away,
    To the high wild woods of laurel
    And the bowers of bay no more.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Edward Housman, titled "September 1922", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
